howItWorks.title: How It Works
howItWorks.subtitle: Three steps. No account creation in between.

step1.title: Choose a template
step1.description: Choose a clean, professional layout. No flashy designs that confuse hiring managers.

step2.title: Fill in your details
step2.description: Add your experience, education, and skills. Real-time preview shows exactly what the PDF will look like.

step3.title: Download your PDF
step3.description: Hit download. Get a properly typeset PDF with selectable text and embedded fonts. Done.

whyBuilt.title: Why This Exists
whyBuilt.bylineRole: Built by an indie engineer · Reachable on GitHub
whyBuilt.paragraph1: Every "free" resume builder pulls the same move: you spend half an hour entering your work history, arranging sections, picking fonts - and right when you hit download, the paywall appears. $3 trial that auto-renews at $24/month. Or a watermark across your name. Or "create an account to continue."
whyBuilt.paragraph2: This tool exists because that shouldn't be normal. Your resume is built entirely in your browser using Typst, a modern typesetting engine. There's no server generating your PDF, which means there's no per-document cost to us - which is why it can actually stay free without tricks.
whyBuilt.paragraph3: No investor pitch deck, no growth metrics to hit, no reason to make you create an account. Just a tool that does the thing.

reallyFree.question: Is this actually free? What's the catch?
reallyFree.answer: There is no catch. The PDF generation happens in your browser using WebAssembly, so we don't pay for server compute per document. Hosting the static site is cheap. That's the whole business model - there isn't one.

dataStorage.question: Where is my data stored?
dataStorage.answer: In your browser's local storage. Your resume data never leaves your device unless you explicitly create an account and opt into cloud sync. We don't track you, we don't run analytics, and we don't sell data.

needAccount.question: Do I need to create an account?
needAccount.answer: No. You can build, edit, and download unlimited resumes without any account. If you want to access your resumes from multiple devices, you can optionally create a free account to sync up to 3 resumes to the cloud. The limit keeps the service free for everyone.

pdfQuality.question: Is the PDF actually good? Or is it a screenshot?
pdfQuality.answer: It's a real PDF with properly typeset text, embedded fonts, and selectable content. We use Typst, a professional typesetting engine (think LaTeX quality without the learning curve). The output is better than what most paid tools produce.

atsFriendly.question: Are the resumes ATS-friendly?
atsFriendly.answer: Yes. The PDFs use clean text rendering with standard fonts. There are no images-of-text, no complex multi-column layouts that confuse parsers, and no invisible elements. ATS systems can read them without issues.

differentFromOthers.question: How is this different from Canva or Zety?
differentFromOthers.answer: Canva requires an account and runs ads. Zety lets you build for free but charges to download. Both store your data on their servers. We don't require accounts, don't charge anything, don't run ads, and keep your data on your device.

offlineWork.question: Does it work offline?
offlineWork.answer: Yes. After loading the site once, the service worker caches everything. You can build and download resumes without an internet connection.

multipleResumes.question: Can I create multiple resumes?
multipleResumes.answer: Yes, unlimited. Create different versions for different job applications, different industries, or different formats. There's no cap.

finalCta.title: Ready to Build Your Resume?
finalCta.subtitle: No signup. Takes about 10 minutes.
finalCta.sub: Free forever. No signup. Takes about 5 minutes.

qa.title: Get Your Questions
qa.subtitle: Straight answers about how the resume builder works, what it costs (nothing), and where your data goes.

isFree.question: Is this actually free? What's the catch?
isFree.answer: There is no catch. The PDF generation happens in your browser using WebAssembly, so we don't pay for server compute per document. Hosting the static site is cheap. That's the whole business model - there isn't one.

signUp.question: Do I need to create an account?
signUp.answer: No. You can build, edit, and download unlimited resumes without any account. If you want to access your resumes from multiple devices, you can optionally create a free account to sync up to 3 resumes to the cloud. The limit keeps the service free for everyone.

pdfExport.question: Is the PDF actually good? Or is it a screenshot?
pdfExport.answer: It's a real PDF with properly typeset text, embedded fonts, and selectable content. We use Typst, a professional typesetting engine - think LaTeX quality without the learning curve. The output is better than what most paid tools produce.

privacy.question: Where is my data stored?
privacy.answer: In your browser's local storage. Your resume data never leaves your device unless you explicitly create an account and opt into cloud sync. We don't track you, we don't run analytics, and we don't sell data.

atsFriendly.question: Are the resumes ATS-friendly?
atsFriendly.answer: Yes. The PDFs use clean text rendering with standard fonts. There are no images-of-text, no complex multi-column layouts that confuse parsers, and no invisible elements. ATS systems can read them without issues.

differentFromOthers.question: How is this different from Canva or Zety?
differentFromOthers.answer: Canva requires an account and shows premium upsells while you build. Zety lets you build for free but charges to download the PDF. Both store your data on their servers. We don't require accounts, don't charge anything, don't run ads, and keep your data on your device.

mobile.question: Does it work on mobile?
mobile.answer: Yes. The builder is fully responsive. On mobile, there's a floating button that opens a full-screen preview so you can see exactly what your resume looks like while editing.

offline.question: Does it work offline?
offline.answer: Yes. After loading the site once, the service worker caches everything. You can build and download resumes without an internet connection.

importExport.question: Can I back up or transfer my resume data?
importExport.answer: Yes. Export your resume as a JSON file and import it on another device or browser. Your data, your file, no account needed.

sections.question: What sections can I add?
sections.answer: Personal info, work experience, education, skills, volunteering, and social links. Each section supports multiple entries. You can drag and drop to reorder sections and individual items within them.

customize.question: Can I change fonts and templates?
customize.answer: Yes. Multiple font options (Roboto, Calibri, Geist, and Noto Naskh Arabic for RTL) and template layouts are available. Pick a template, pick a font, and the preview updates instantly.

cloudSync.question: How does cloud sync work?
cloudSync.answer: Create a free account, and your resumes sync automatically across devices. The free tier supports up to 3 synced resumes. Local resumes on your device are always unlimited - cloud sync is just for cross-device access.

browsers.question: What browsers are supported?
browsers.answer: Chrome, Firefox, Safari, and Edge - any modern browser that supports WebAssembly. Use the latest version for the best experience.

support.question: How do I report a bug or request a feature?
support.answer: Open an issue on our GitHub repository. The project is open source - you can also contribute directly if you want.
